data.title: SEC Considers Framework to Shield Bitcoin from Quantum Computing
data.description:
The proposal, titled the Post-Quantum Financial Infrastructure Framework (PQFIF), was submitted to the U. S.
source: https://coindoo.com/sec-considers-framework-to-shield-bitcoin-from-quantum-computing/

data.title: ECB says Digital Euro necessary payments during major disruptions
data.description:
The European Central Bank (ECB) has insisted on a digital euro to ensure payment stability during major disruptions.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/ecb-digital-euro-is-necessary-payments/

data.title: Hong Kong Firm Plans Major Investment in Quantum Company
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Hong Kong-listed company Ying Universe (stock code 03700) has announced its intention to purchase 168.5 million shares of Guofu Quantum at a price of HKD 1.78 per share.

Previously, Foresight News reported that Guofu Quantum Innovation Co., Ltd., through its subsidiary Fuqiang Equity Investment Management Co., Ltd., established the 'Guofu Tokenized Digital Asset Limited Partnership Fund' (RWA8 Fund).

data.title: Eric Trump posts a picture hinting at a major announcement tomorrow
data.description: Trump's second son Eric Trump posted a picture hinting that a major announcement will be made tomorrow, but the specific content has not been disclosed yet.

data.title: Ethereum Flipping Bitcoin Is Just ‘Ridiculous’
data.description:
Last week, Ethereum cofounder and ConsenSys CEO Joseph Lubin took to Twitter to comment on an interview with Tom Lee, whose company BitMine (the MicroStrategy of Ethereum) is now the largest holder of ETH, with 1. 86 million coins.
source: https://www.thecoinrepublic.com/2025/09/04/ethereum-flipping-bitcoin-is-just-ridiculous/

data.title: Crypto-friendly bank Lead Bank completes $70 million financing round, led by a16z and others
data.description: Crypto-friendly bank Lead Bank has raised $70 million at a $1.47 billion valuation. A16z and Khosla Ventures led the round, with participation from existing investors Ribbit Capital, Coatue, and Zeev Ventures, as well as new investors Iconiq Capital and Greycroft. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Ukraine Receives Bitcoin Transfer Worth Over $1 Million
data.description: According to Foresight News, Whale Alert has detected a transaction involving a Ukraine-related address receiving 10 bitcoins approximately 10 minutes ago. The value of this transfer is estimated to be around $1.108 million.

data.title: Trump signs an executive order enforcing Japan trade agreement
data.description:
Trump signed an executive order to implement the U. S. -Japan trade deal agreed in July.
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-signs-order-enforcing-japan-trade-deal/

data.title: In the past 7 hours, Bitmine and several on-chain whales have hoarded over 827,000 ETH
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, Bitmine and several on-chain whales have accumulated over 827,000 ETH. Bitmine received 48,225 ETH worth $207.54 million from BitGo. A newly created wallet, "0xAF 8," withdrew 12,692 ETH worth $54.84 million from OKX. A newly created wallet, "0xC 77," received 18,404 ETH worth $79.45 million from FalconX. Another newly created wallet received 3,388 ETH worth $14.63 million from FalconX, bringing its total holdings to 18,388 ETH (approximately $79.14 million).

data.title: Trader Opens WLFI Long Position Following Blacklist Incident
data.description: According to Foresight News, a trader known as '麻吉大哥' has initiated a long position in WLFI after the address associated with Sun Yuchen was blacklisted. The trader currently holds 10 million WLFI tokens, valued at approximately $1.82 million, with an average entry price of $0.1822 per token.

data.title: Huang Licheng Opens Significant WLFI Position Following Justin Sun Address Blacklisting
data.description: According to BlockBeats, on-chain analyst Ai Yi has reported that Huang Licheng, also known as 'Machi Big Brother,' initiated a significant WLFI position after Justin Sun's address was blacklisted by WLFI. Huang Licheng opened a fivefold WLFI long position, currently holding 10 million tokens valued at $1.82 million, with an opening price of $0.1822 per token.

data.title: SEC Proposes Safe Harbor for Cryptocurrency, Eases Broker-Dealer Rules
data.description:
SEC introduces a proposed safe harbor framework to reform crypto regulations, impact on broker-dealer rules in the U. S.
source: https://coincu.com/360079-sec-safe-harbor-crypto-rules

data.title: REX-Osprey is expected to launch the first DOGE spot ETF next week
data.description: REX Shares and Osprey Funds may launch the first ETF that directly invests in DOGE spot as early as next week. REX-Osprey filed a prospectus with the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Wednesday for the REX-Osprey DOGE ETF, which will trade under the ticker symbol DOJE. The prospectus also includes ETFs related to Trump, XRP, and BONK. (The Block)

data.title: An Ethereum ICO participant who has been silent for 8 years pledged 150,000 ETH, worth $656 million
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, an Ethereum IC 0 participant who has been silent for 8 years has pledged 150,000 ETH on Ethereum, worth US$656 million.

data.title: Lead Bank Secures $70 Million Funding to Expand Fintech Operations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, Lead Bank, known for its crypto-friendly approach, has successfully raised $70 million in funding, valuing the bank at $1.47 billion. The funding round was led by a16z and Khosla Ventures, with participation from existing investors Ribbit Capital, Coatue, and Zeev Ventures. New investors include Iconiq Capital and Greycroft.

The newly acquired funds will be utilized to strengthen Lead Bank's balance sheet, aiding its expansion in the fintech sector. Lead Bank's clientele includes Affirm Holdings Inc., a 'buy now, pay later' company, Ramp Business Corporation, a corporate expense management platform, and Bridge, a stablecoin business unit recently acquired by Stripe Inc.

data.title: Figma Reports 41% Revenue Growth in Second Quarter
data.description: According to BlockBeats, U.S. design software developer Figma has announced its financial results for the second quarter, revealing a total revenue of approximately $249.6 million, marking a 41% increase compared to the same period last year. As of June 30, the company holds around $1.6 billion in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities, which includes $90.8 million in Bitcoin spot ETFs.

data.title: Trump signs executive order formally implementing US-Japan trade agreement
data.description: The White House announced that US President Trump signed an executive order formally implementing the US-Japan trade agreement. The order will reportedly clarify tariff adjustments to ensure that Japanese imports previously subject to higher tariffs will not be subject to double taxation, while goods previously subject to tariffs below 15% will be adjusted to the new rates. Separate industry-specific treatment will also be implemented for automobiles and auto parts, aerospace products, generic pharmaceuticals, and natural resources that cannot be naturally obtained or produced in the United States. The statement also stated that Japan will provide breakthrough market access to key sectors for US manufacturers in manufacturing, aerospace, agriculture, food, energy, automobiles, and industrial products. The Japanese government is working to accelerate the implementation of its "minimum access" rice program, increasing US rice purchases by 75%, and purchasing US agricultural products, including corn, soybeans, fertilizers, bioethanol, and other US products, totaling $8 billion annually. (Jinshi)

data.title: Trump administration files new argument seeking court support for removal of Fed Governor Cook
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice has made new arguments for why President Trump should be allowed to remove Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook, calling her claim of a "pretext for interest rate cuts" unfounded. Cook, accused of mortgage fraud, is currently challenging her dismissal. On Thursday, U.S. government lawyers again urged a judge to reject Cook's request to block her removal pending litigation, reinforcing arguments from last week's hearing. The filing came hours after reports that the Justice Department had launched a criminal investigation into Cook. The U.S. government argues that the fraud allegations, first made by Federal Housing Finance Agency Director Pulte, constitute sufficient "cause" for Trump to fire her under U.S. law. In Thursday's filing, the Justice Department emphasized that the judge cannot "question" Trump's determination that there is grounds for dismissal, and again rejected his claim that the dismissal was a pretext for taking control of the Federal Reserve and cutting interest rates. "Her only 'evidence' is that the President has criticized Fed policy," the filing states. "But policy disagreements alone do not warrant the President's dismissal." (Jin Shi)

data.title: Fed's Goolsbee: Labor market may be deteriorating, inflation could pick up again
data.description: Fed's Goolsbee said the labor market may be deteriorating and inflation may pick up again.

data.title: Trump: Expecting strong employment figures within a year
data.description: US President Trump said he expects employment data to be very strong within a year. (Jinshi)
